name: checks-superstaq
documentation:
# checks-superstaq
Check script tools for maintaining code quality and hygiene.

modules.name: checks_superstaq.build_docs
documentation: !/usr/bin/env python3

functions.name: checks_superstaq.build_docs.run
documentation:
Checks that the docs build successfully.

Args:
    *args: Command line arguments.

Returns:
    Terminal exit code. 0 indicates success, while any other integer indicates a test failure.

modules.name: checks_superstaq.format_
documentation: !/usr/bin/env python3

functions.name: checks_superstaq.format_.run
documentation:
Runs black and isort on the repository (formatting check).

Args:
    *args: Command line arguments.
    include: Glob(s) indicating which tracked files to consider (e.g. "*.py").
    exclude: Glob(s) indicating which tracked files to skip (e.g. "*integration_test.py").
    silent: If True, restrict printing to warning and error messages.

Returns:
    Terminal exit code. 0 indicates success, while any other integer indicates a test failure.

modules.name: checks_superstaq.pytest_
documentation: !/usr/bin/env python3

functions.name: checks_superstaq.pytest_.run
documentation:
Runs pytest on the repository.

Args:
    *args: Command line arguments.
    include: Glob(s) indicating which tracked files to consider (e.g. "*.py").
    exclude: Glob(s) indicating which tracked files to skip (e.g. "*integration_test.py").
    integration_setup: Optional function to run before integration tests (for example,
        to set environmental variables).
    silent: If True, restrict printing to warning and error messages.

Returns:
    Terminal exit code. 0 indicates success, while any other integer indicates a test failure.

modules.name: checks_superstaq.pylint_import_annotations
documentation: Check for whether python file has from __future__ import annotations as the first import.

functions.name: checks_superstaq.pylint_import_annotations.register
documentation:
Registers plugin to be accessed by pylint.

Args:
    linter: The base pylinter which the custom checker will inherit from.

classes.name: checks_superstaq.pylint_import_annotations.ImportAnnotationsChecker

methods.name: checks_superstaq.pylint_import_annotations.ImportAnnotationsChecker.visit_module
documentation: Sets the flag for import annotations found to False for each module.

methods.name: checks_superstaq.pylint_import_annotations.ImportAnnotationsChecker.visit_importfrom
documentation:
Checks if 'from __future__ import annotations' is one of the imports in a module.

Args:
    node: All the ImportFrom nodes in a module.

methods.name: checks_superstaq.pylint_import_annotations.ImportAnnotationsChecker.leave_module
documentation:
Method reports if 'from __future__ import annotations' was found in a file that does not
start with '_'.

Args:
    node: The current module.
Checker for whether future annotations is imported in python files.

documentation: !/usr/bin/env python3

documentation:
Runs mypy on the repository (typing check).

Args:

Returns:

modules.name: checks_superstaq.pylint_init_return
documentation: Check return type annotation of __init__ functions.

functions.name: checks_superstaq.pylint_init_return.register
documentation:
Registers plugin to be accessed by pylint.

Args:
    linter: The base pylinter which the custom checker will inherit from.

classes.name: checks_superstaq.pylint_init_return.InitChecker

methods.name: checks_superstaq.pylint_init_return.InitChecker.visit_functiondef
documentation:
Called for function and method definitions (def).

Checks if the return type annotation for __init__ is explicity None or incorrect.

Args:
    node: Node for a function or method definition in the AST.

Checker for return type annotations for class constructors (__init__).

* Check that all __init__ functions have the return type annotation '-> None'.

Runs 'ruff check' on the repository (formatting check).

modules.name: checks_superstaq.coverage_
documentation: !/usr/bin/env python3

functions.name: checks_superstaq.coverage_.run
documentation:
Checks to make sure that all code is covered by unit tests.

Args:
    *args: Command line arguments.
    include: Glob(s) indicating which tracked files to consider (e.g. "*.py").
    exclude: Glob(s) indicating which tracked files to skip (e.g. "*integration_test.py").

Returns:
    Terminal exit code. 0 indicates success, while any other integer indicates a test failure.

functions.name: checks_superstaq.coverage_._run_on_files
documentation: Helper function to run coverage tests on the given files with the given pytest arguments.

modules.name: checks_superstaq.check_utils
documentation: Dumping ground for check script utilities.

functions.name: checks_superstaq.check_utils._check_output
documentation: Wrapper for subprocess.check_output to run commands from root_dir and clean up the output.

functions.name: checks_superstaq.check_utils.styled
documentation:
Style (format) text for printing in the console.

Args:
    text: The text to style.
    style_code: Console code for the text style.

Returns:
    Styled text.

functions.name: checks_superstaq.check_utils.warning
documentation:
Style (format) text indicating a warning.

Args:
    text: The text to style.

Returns:
    Styled text.

functions.name: checks_superstaq.check_utils.failure
documentation:
Style (format) text indicating a failure.

Args:
    text: The text to style.

Returns:
    Styled text.

functions.name: checks_superstaq.check_utils.success
documentation:
Style (format) text indicating success.

Args:
    text: The text to style.

Returns:
    Styled text.

functions.name: checks_superstaq.check_utils.get_tracked_files
documentation:
Identify all files tracked by git (in this repo) that match the given match_patterns.

If no patterns are provided, return a list of all tracked files in the repo.

Args:
    include: The string of patterns to match files to.

Returns:
    Output of `subprocess.check_output` wrapper.
methods for identifying files to check

functions.name: checks_superstaq.check_utils.existing_files
documentation:
Returns the subset of `files` which actually exist.

Args:
    files: The files to check existence of.

Returns:
    A list of existing files.

functions.name: checks_superstaq.check_utils.exclude_files
documentation:
Returns the files which don't match any of the globs in exclude.

Args:
    files: The files to check for exclusion.
    exclude: The files to exclude.

Returns:
    The excluded files.

functions.name: checks_superstaq.check_utils.select_files
documentation:
Returns the files which match at least one of the globs in include.

Args:
    files: The files to check for matching with inclusion pattern.
    include: The patterns to match for selecting input files.

Returns:
    The selected files.

functions.name: checks_superstaq.check_utils.get_changed_files
documentation:
Returns the files that have been changed in the current branch.

You can specify git revisions to compare against when determining whether a file is considered
to have "changed".  If multiple revisions are provided, this script compares against their most
recent common ancestor.

If an empty list of revisions is specified, this script will default to the first of the
default_branches (specified above) that it finds.  If none of these branches exists, this method
raises a ValueError.

Args:
    files: The files to check for changes in.
    revisions: The git revisions to compare against for file checking.
    silent: An indicator for whether to print additional info about common ancestors.

Returns:
    A list of files that were changed in the current branch.

Raises:
    ValueError: If there are any invalid revision arguments.

functions.name: checks_superstaq.check_utils._get_ancestor
documentation: Helper function to identify the most recent common ancestor of the given git revisions.

functions.name: checks_superstaq.check_utils._revision_exists
documentation: Helper function to check whether a git revision exists.

functions.name: checks_superstaq.check_utils.get_test_files
documentation:
For the given files, identify all associated test files.

I.e. test files are those with the same name, but with a "_test.py" suffix or "test_" prefix.

Args:
    files: The list of files to search for associated test files of.
    exclude: The files to exclude in the search.
    silent: An indicator of whether or not to add additional prints.

Returns:
    A list of test files corresponding to the input files.

functions.name: checks_superstaq.check_utils.get_check_parser
documentation:
Construct a command-line argument parser common to all check scripts.

This parser collects a list of files to check, with "no files" == "all files".

In addition, this parser has flags/arguments to:
- skip certain (specified) checks.
- run incremental checks, i.e., on the files that have changed since a specified git revision,
- exclude files matching a specified glob.

Args:
    no_files: Ignore file-related arguments.

Returns:
    A console argument parser.

functions.name: checks_superstaq.check_utils.extract_files
documentation:
Collect a list of files to test, according to command line arguments and `include`/`exclude`
values.

Args:
    parsed_args: The namespace generated by the ArgumentParser returned by `get_check_parser()`.
    include: Glob(s) indicating which tracked files to consider (e.g. "*.py").
    exclude: Glob(s) indicating which tracked files to skip (e.g. "*integration_test.py").
    silent: If True, restrict printing to warning and error messages.

Returns:
    If `parsed_args.files` is empty (i.e. no file paths or globs have been passed to the file
    parser), a list of tracked files in the active repo branch meeting all of the following
    criteria:
    1. The file's path (relative to `root_dir`) matches at least one path or glob in `include`,
    2. The path does not match any path or glob in `exclude`,
    3. The path does not match any path or glob in `parsed_args.exclude`,
    4. If `parsed_args.revisions` is not None, the file additionally must have been modified in
        the current branch (see `get_changed_files()` for details on how this is determined).

    If `parsed_args.files` is nonempty, a restricted file list containing:
    1. Paths meeting the above criteria and matching any glob in `parsed_args.files`.
    2. Paths meeting the above criteria and located in any extant subdirectory passed to the
        file parser directly.
    3. Paths to extant files passed to the file parser directly, regardless of above criteria.

functions.name: checks_superstaq.check_utils.enable_exit_on_failure
documentation:
Decorator optionally allowing a function to exit instead of failing.

I.e., catches a failing return code for safely exiting function.

Args:
    func_with_returncode: A function with a return code for failure.

Returns:
    A decorated function that handles failing return codes.

classes.name: checks_superstaq.check_utils.Style
documentation: Container for string formatting console codes.
container for string formatting console codes

modules.name: checks_superstaq.requirements
documentation: !/usr/bin/env python3

functions.name: checks_superstaq.requirements.run
documentation:
Checks that:
- all pip requirements files (i.e. files matching *requirements.txt) are sorted
- all upstream packages are pinned to their latest versions

Args:
    *args: Command line arguments.
    include: Glob(s) indicating which tracked files to consider (e.g. "*.py").
    exclude: Glob(s) indicating which tracked files to skip (e.g. "*integration_test.py").
    upstream_match: String to match package name and version.
    silent: If True, restrict printing to warning and error messages.

Returns:
    Terminal exit code. 0 indicates success, while any other integer indicates a test failure.

functions.name: checks_superstaq.requirements._check_package_versions
documentation:
Check whether package requirements matching 'match' are up-to-date with their latest
versions.
Print warnings if matching requirements are out of date.  Return whether the requirements file
*must* be updated, i.e., return 'True' iff packages are out of date and 'strict == True'.

functions.name: checks_superstaq.requirements._get_latest_version
documentation: Retrieve the latest version of a package.

functions.name: checks_superstaq.requirements._get_local_version
documentation: Retrieve the local version of a package (if installed).

functions.name: checks_superstaq.requirements._get_pypi_version
documentation: Retrieve the latest version of a package on PyPI (if found).

functions.name: checks_superstaq.requirements._inspect_local_version
documentation: Check that the package is installed with the same minor version (X.Y.*) as latest_version.

modules.name: checks_superstaq.configs
documentation: !/usr/bin/env python3

functions.name: checks_superstaq.configs.run
documentation:
Checks that check-script configurations are consistent across repos.

Args:

    silent: If True, restrict printing to warning and error messages.

Returns:
    Terminal exit code. 0 indicates success, while any other integer indicates a test failure.

functions.name: checks_superstaq.configs._trim_lines
documentation: Remove package-specific configuration text, and identify the starting lines to compare.

functions.name: checks_superstaq.configs._announce_diff
documentation: Announce a difference between configuration files.

functions.name: checks_superstaq.configs._line_text
documentation: Identify one or multiple line numbers.
